conflicts.description: Chapter 16 retells Maren's zero event as a row-house collapse south of the Margins, while the opening, Vel's chapter 9 explanation, and the route journal consistently treat the Scour as the threshold site tied to Maren's crossing.
conflicts.canonical_value: Maren crossed at the Scour threshold eight years earlier.
conflicts.resolution_note: The Scour reading is repeated in the opening setup, Vel's first disclosure to Kem, and Maren's own journal map, and it is the load-bearing assumption behind the entire southbound plot. The row-house version appears once, would force broad reinterpretation of the route journal and climax, and conflicts with the existing sheet and the cycle context pack, so it is logged as manuscript drift rather than adopted as canon.

conflicts.description: Vel's chapter 1 belief that two synchronized descents should hold the threshold conflicts with Commander Osen's chapter 17 briefing and the chapter 18 crossing mechanics after site dampening.
conflicts.canonical_value: At story present, the damped Scour threshold cannot be relied on with only two contributors; the successful crossing requires Vel's terminal spend, Kem's terminal spend, and Sable's seven-level assist from outside the mouth.
conflicts.resolution_note: Vel's earlier arithmetic is based on an eight-year-old observation and incomplete information. Osen has the current classified site report, and chapter 18 confirms her version in action, so the sheet keeps Vel's old model as character belief only and updates world-level canon to the degraded three-contributor condition.
